sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.112 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$$be a hereditary saturated formation and let${ w}^{*}$denote the class of all finite groups$G$for which$(G) ( )$and the normalizers of all Sylow subgroups of$G$are$$-subnormal in$G$. It is known that${ w}^{*}$is a formation. Must${ w}^{*}$be a~saturated formation? { A.\,F.\,Vasil'ev, T.\,I.\,Vasil'eva}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
